channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Congressman questions whether state bill could’ve changed outcome at FSU
item.description: Rep. Randy Fine, who was recently elected to Congress, is revisiting a discussion about guns on college campuses, following Thursday's deadly mass shooting at Florida State University.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court orders immigration officials to restore legal status of three people who came to Texas on student visas
item.description: The three former students went to UT-Arlington and UT-Dallas. While they have prevailed in court so far, many other international students’ education futures remain uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge says detained Tufts student must be transferred from Louisiana to Vermont
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that a Tufts University student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities in March be brought to Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon's week of power struggles: Leak fallout and shouting matches hit Hegseth's inner circle
item.description: Multiple defense officials describe a bitter internal feud behind the sudden ouster of three aides to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth — with finger-pointing and accusations flying.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Abrego Garcia case pulls Democrats into the immigration debate Trump wants to have
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For Democrats, the Kilmar Abrego Garcia case is about fundamental American ideals — due process, following court orders, preventing government overreach. For the Trump administration and Republicans, it's about foreigners and gang threats and danger in American towns and cities.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge orders new limits on DOGE data access at Social Security Administration
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland granted a preliminary injunction that bars DOGE staffers from accessing non-anonymized personal data at the Social Security Administration.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ethics investigation of state Senate president prompts conflict-of-interest rules scrutiny
item.description: Attorney and former State DFL House Majority Leader Ryan Winkler joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition on Thursday to talk about conflicts of interest at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. will withdraw about 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to work with Kurdish allies to counter the Islamic State group, a U.S. official said Thursday. The official spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss details not yet announced publicly. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota officials post strong March jobs report, note economic uncertainty ahead
item.description: Economic officials reported job growth in March but noted that mass layoff data likely isn’t represented yet.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: PIX11 selected as NYC debate sponsor for first time
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – For the first time, PIX11 has been chosen by the New York City Campaign Finance Board to be a debate sponsor this year. PIX11 has been granted the opportunity to host seven debates for the 2025 citywide elections.
item.dc:creator: PIX11 Web Team, PIX 11

item.title: Former DeSantis spokesperson accused of exposing himself at mall
item.description: A New College of Florida official and former spokesperson for Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis was arrested Tuesday and charged with exposure of sexual organs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Clashing Over Campus Control: Lawmakers vs. DeSantis on transparency
item.description: For years, lawmakers have speculated that the leaders who run the state's Universities and Colleges have been gifted these positions based on their political loyalties to the Governor.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supervisor Lawson-Remer presents 'local battleplan' during State of County address
item.description: This year’s state of the county address was delivered in Balboa Park by acting Chair Terra Lawson-Remer. She shared her proposed plan for how to move the county forward in challenging fiscal times and what she considers “a moment of democratic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration plans to end free tax filing program
item.description: But, in mid-March, the IRS staff assigned to the program were told to stop working on it for the 2026 tax filing season, the two sources told the AP.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi River named 'most endangered' by conservation group, cites FEMA cuts
item.description: The organization said the federal government plays a key role in protecting the river and helping homeowners prepare for, and rebuild after, major flooding.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Protest of Lombardo's 'freeloaders' comment planned Saturday in downtown Las Vegas
item.description: A rally planned Saturday afternoon in downtown Las Vegas is stepping up the pushback against Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's remarks that freeloaders are behind opposition to policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin squeaks past Minnesota for the top spot in voter turnout
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State confirms Wisconsin narrowly surpassed Minnesota with voter turnout in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Cuomo housing plan faces scrutiny
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor are calling out former Gov. Andrew Cuomo for his housing plan. They claim it is light on details and that part of it was written using ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: White House doctor says Trump is 'fully fit' in medical report
item.description: President Trump lost 20 pounds, lowered “bad” cholesterol and has “diverticulosis and a benign polyp,” according to a medical report released by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News
